text: Capstone thesis: mathematics' global structure is recoverable from formal proofs with NO supervision - Leiden clustering of Mathlib's dependency graph reproduces named domains (a-0001), and an in-degree-filtered cross-cluster premise criterion detects operational bridge theorems (a-0007) that align with historically celebrated connections (a-0008). This operationalizes one notion of mathematical interestingness (bridges) and identifies SetTheory as the integrative hub (a-0010). The central open problem is the gap between operational bridges (shared premises, detected) and conceptual bridges (shared proof structure, undetected; a-0009). Bridge-aware proving (a-0011) is the proposed but untested payoff.

comments.placeholders: The paper's 'compressibility' framing leans on a placeholder reference (compression2024, bibliographic details = TODO); authors/affiliations are also placeholders. Reasoning content is unaffected.
